Video.summary: Meet the earliest civilizations of the Tigris-Euphrates, Nile, and Indus River valleys.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Origins of Great Ancient Civilizations
Video.title: Cradles of Civilization

Video.summary: Here, explore the economic, social, and religious life of the Sumerians.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Origins of Great Ancient Civilizations
Video.title: First Cities of Sumer

Video.summary: A look at kings, scribes, and soldiers illuminates the creation of political institutions.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Origins of Great Ancient Civilizations
Video.title: Mesopotamian Kings and Scribes

Video.summary: Examine the career and kingdom of Hammurabi, King of Babylon.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Origins of Great Ancient Civilizations
Video.title: Hammurabi's Babylon

Video.summary: Focus on the so-called early dynastic period and the Old Kingdom.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Origins of Great Ancient Civilizations
Video.title: Egypt in the Pyramid Age

Video.summary: Examine a period of Egyptian history contemporaneous with Hammurabi\'s Babylon.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Origins of Great Ancient Civilizations
Video.title: The Middle Kingdom

Video.summary: Egypt\'s monarchy dominated the Near East until the "Sea Peoples" attacked.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Origins of Great Ancient Civilizations
Video.title: Imperial Egypt

Video.summary: Take a closer look at three areas influenced by the early river valley civilizations.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Origins of Great Ancient Civilizations
Video.title: New Peoples of the Bronze Age

Image.alt: New Peoples of the Bronze Age
New Peoples of the Bronze Age
New Peoples of the Bronze Age

Video.summary: Examine the historical origins of the Greeks and Romans, which is still an open question among scholars.
Video.grandparentTitle: Greece and Rome: An Integrated History of the Ancient Mediterranean
Video.title: Who Were the Greeks? Who Were the Romans?

Image.alt: Who Were the Greeks? Who Were the Romans?
Who Were the Greeks? Who Were the Romans?
Who Were the Greeks? Who Were the Romans?

Video.summary: Trade and travel were widespread throughout the Mediterranean basin from early times onward.
Video.grandparentTitle: Greece and Rome: An Integrated History of the Ancient Mediterranean
Video.title: Trade and Travel in the Mediterranean

Image.alt: Trade and Travel in the Mediterranean
Trade and Travel in the Mediterranean
Trade and Travel in the Mediterranean

Video.summary: Both Greeks and Romans had democratic tendencies and a predisposition to favor the aristocracy.
Video.grandparentTitle: Greece and Rome: An Integrated History of the Ancient Mediterranean
Video.title: Democratic or Republican

Video.summary: The Greeks first established trial by jury; the Romans established the principle that law is organic.
Video.grandparentTitle: Greece and Rome: An Integrated History of the Ancient Mediterranean
Video.title: Law and Order

Video.summary: Both the Greeks and Romans denied full human status to those at the bottom of the social ladder.
Video.grandparentTitle: Greece and Rome: An Integrated History of the Ancient Mediterranean
Video.title: Less than Fully Human

Video.summary: From very early, the Romans were subject to Greek influence from Greek colonists and the Etruscans.
Video.grandparentTitle: Greece and Rome: An Integrated History of the Ancient Mediterranean
Video.title: Close Encounters, 750 - 272 B.C.

Video.summary: After defeating Carthage in the Second Punic War, Rome turned its attention to the eastern Mediterranean.
Video.grandparentTitle: Greece and Rome: An Integrated History of the Ancient Mediterranean
Video.title: The Velvet Glove, 272 - 190 B.C.

Video.summary: Greek religion made a deep impact on the Romans, whose animistic beliefs differed from the Greeks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Greece and Rome: An Integrated History of the Ancient Mediterranean
Video.title: How the Two Polytheisms (Almost) Merged

Video.summary: By conquering Greece, Philip II of Macedon set the stage for the rise of his son, Alexander the Great.
Video.grandparentTitle: Alexander the Great and the Hellenistic Age
Video.title: Greeks and Macedonians

Video.summary: Alexander's path was prepared by Philip, but few could predict how the son would eclipse the father.
Video.grandparentTitle: Alexander the Great and the Hellenistic Age
Video.title: Alexander the Divine?

Video.summary: Did his Egyptian sojourn teach Alexander the political value of blurring the lines between human and divine?
Video.grandparentTitle: Alexander the Great and the Hellenistic Age
Video.title: The Blazing Star

Video.summary: Can we discern the historical Alexander, or has the myth swallowed the man?
Video.grandparentTitle: Alexander the Great and the Hellenistic Age
Video.title: Alexander—Myth and Reality

Video.summary: Look at "Operation Torch" as a study in using the military as an instrument of policy.
Video.grandparentTitle: Masters of War: History's Greatest Strategic Thinkers
Video.title: Why Strategy Matters

Video.summary: Take a systematic look at what makes Thucydides perhaps history's first great strategist.
Video.grandparentTitle: Masters of War: History's Greatest Strategic Thinkers
Video.title: Thucydides on Strategy

Video.summary: Join the lively debate over the efficacy of the Sicilian Expedition and the Melian Dialogue.
Video.grandparentTitle: Masters of War: History's Greatest Strategic Thinkers
Video.title: Thucydides as a Possession for All Time

Image.alt: Thucydides as a Possession for All Time
Thucydides as a Possession for All Time
Thucydides as a Possession for All Time

Video.summary: Examine the historical context for "Master Sun's Military Methods."
Video.grandparentTitle: Masters of War: History's Greatest Strategic Thinkers
Video.title: Sun Tzu's The Art of War

Image.alt: Sun Tzu's The Art of War
Sun Tzu's The Art of War
Sun Tzu's The Art of War
